commentary: Campaign plan: the Confederate exit economy (10 VP/CSP through 0101/0111, contingent on a clear LOC road) is their biggest account - I will physically hold both exit hexes and the road ring (0201/0110/0211) at game end to void it entirely, while garrisoning 0211 and 0502 to deny their 40 VP of occupation objectives. GT1: 112/113 seize the two 20-VP hexes now; 104/105/106 begin the march to the exit fortress; 101/102 form on 1115 and 103 on 1108 as a delay line; 110/111 fall back from the exposed south onto 1115; cavalry 109 escapes west before Forrest pins it; Wilder pulls northwest as the fortress reserve; 108 milks the 0822 credit until pressured.

The southern mass came up the center; 110/111 sprint the last two hexes into the 1115 position before Gracie pins them, forming an 18-strength stack with 101/102. 106 takes over the 1108 garrison; 109 (cavalry) and the exposed 114 both fall back on 1108 to screen against the Forrest/Armstrong cavalry group. Fortress march continues: 112 to exit 0111, 105 to exit 0101, 104 to road-ring 0201, 103 to road-ring 0110, Wilder to 0211. 113 stands on 0502. Reinforcements enter automatically this turn and get orders next turn.

148 died on the south-edge entry hexes - all future arrivals get the same standing order: run northwest to 0211 before the mass eats them. 110/111 are ZOC-locked at 1017 and must stand. The 1115 and 1108 stacks hold one more turn against the approaching eastern mass and cavalry; 108 abandons 0822 while the credit is still Union (occupation only flips if they physically pass through). Fortress: Wilder closes on 0211 with 114 and 108 as follow-on garrison, 105 heads for exit 0101, 104 for ring hex 0201, 103 for ring hex 0110; 112 stands on exit 0111, 113 on 0502.

General withdrawal into the fortress. The delay line has bought three turns; before the central mass makes contact, 101/102 quit 1115 for ring hex 0110 and 106 quits 1108 for 0201 - the occupation credits stay Union unless the enemy physically passes through, and they cannot cash exits or the 20-VP hexes if the ring holds. 110/111 slipped their pin and escape north. 105 garrisons exit 0101, 104 ring hex 0201, 114 stacks Wilder on 0211 (13 strength on their biggest prize), 108 stacks 113 on 0502, cavalry 109 becomes the mobile reserve at 0506, 147 keeps running for 0211. Off-map reinforcements stay bottled while the enemy stands on the entry hexes - they will be a gift behind his army if he moves north.

Ring consolidation before contact: 101/102 march to reinforce 0502 (their west wing's 50 CSP is aimed there), 108 stacks Wilder on 0211, 114 and 106 thicken ring hex 0201 with 104, 110/111 join 103 on ring hex 0110, 105 garrisons exit 0101, cavalry 109 falls back to interior reserve 0305. In their rear: 149 and artillery 150 retake the now-empty 0822 - the occupation flips back Union the moment we pass through - and the XX Corps enters the vacated south this turn for a second-front sweep next turn.

Paying for greed: 149/150 died freelancing at 0822 - noted for the doctrine book. Emergency repairs: Wilder and 108 step back onto empty 0211 before the probes claim it; 113 abandons 0502 rather than feed a 5:1 assault (occupation scores at game END - the policy AI conveyors on rather than garrisons, so a late retake is live); 106 and 105 garrison the naked exit 0101; 101/102/111 form the interior reserve at 0305 able to plug any face; 109 breaks from Scott if the ZOC allows. Exits 0111 (112+110) and ring 0110/0201 hold. Their cavalry is loose in my north - reserves cover the roads.

Their west wing is fly-papered in the south chasing entry-hex snacks; only the center mass and cavalry are actually at my walls. 147 joins Wilder/108 on 0211 (18 strength on their 20-VP prize - exchanges there are VP-neutral and burn their clock). 111 plugs the north road at 0105 against the cavalry sweep; 114 and 109 tuck inside the ring away from Scott's pounce range; 151 runs the southwest corner route home. All ring garrisons stand: exits 0101/0111, ring 0201/0110, prize 0211. The supply train enters the south this turn - routing it home is next turn's problem; the fly-paper wing is between it and safety.

Full convergence on the fortress; brace and keep the retake cards in hand. 101/102 tighten from 0308 to 0209 before the east face gets 3:1'd; 114 gives the north face depth at 0104 against the Forrest/Gist/Scott group; 109 slips to 0206 if the ZOC allows. 147 stands at 0220 and Wilder/108 at 0112 - both adjacent to empty 0211, ready for the night-turn reoccupation (GT9 has no combat, so a night retake cannot be answered until GT10). All ring garrisons hold. Their fly-paper detachment - ten units - still guards entry hexes against an off-map corps.

Night repositioning. Wilder and 108 take the naked exit 0111 (13 strength - the LOC void depends on holding both exits at the end); 0211 cannot be re-entered tonight (enemy artillery ZOC blocks night entry) so it becomes a late-game daylight operation. 101/102 plug the 0210 corridor behind the ring; cavalry 109 to 0205 as the northern fire brigade against Fulton/Liddell already inside my outskirts; 147 marches home up the west edge, clear of their assault columns. All garrisons hold: 0101, 0201, 0110, 0105. Their army is fully committed here while 0502, 0822, 1108 and 1115 sit empty behind them.

147 keeps its 33-CSP entourage marching in circles - run north, stay one hex ahead. 154 trails the chase column up the west edge as the second flipper. 103 thickens ring hex 0110 to 15 strength against the 40-CSP east-face assault. Everything else stands: exits 0101 (9) and 0111 (10) with Wilder's 13 backstopping at 0112, ring 0201 (9), north picket 0105. 0211 is still credited Union - nobody has passed through it - so denial, not retake, is the play there. Endgame: when the XX Corps finally enters, it sweeps the empty hexes behind their army, including my 30 VP at 1920/2311.

The GT10 pocket battle cost me 110/112 under the fixed rules - the price of a sealed pocket, honestly paid. Refill the naked exit: 102/108 step onto 0111 (10 strength), Wilder backstops at 0112. 147 tries to slip its seven pursuers one more time - every turn they chase it is a turn they don't assault. All other garrisons stand: 0101 (9), 0201 (9), 0110 (10), 0105 picket (9), cavalry at 0106. When the XX Corps enters the vacated south, it forms two task forces: east to retake my 30 VP at 1920/2311 plus 1115/1108, west for 0822 and 0502.

The fortress repulsed the assault and killed Anderson6; every garrison stands. Their army is in three pieces: 30 CSP chasing 147, 45 CSP back south farming entry hexes, only 35 CSP at my walls. 147 sprints the last stretch home to 0113. Everything else holds. When the XX Corps enters: cavalry rides east for my 30 VP at 1920 and 2311, infantry columns take 1115/1108 and 0822, the train runs the gauntlet for the exits under standing doctrine.

Denial endgame. The XX Corps is stalled off-map behind a blocked column head - no sweep. What remains wins margins: both exits garrisoned (their 10-VP-per-CSP economy stays at zero), 0211 stays poisoned by the ring so it never flips, 147 completes its escape to 0113 after leading thirty CSP around the map for six turns. Hold everything.

Penultimate turn. 147 steps home to 0113 inside the ring after its six-turn odyssey. 159 sits quiet at 1128 - it cannot reach a scoring hex in the time left, so it will not gift five CSP to their kill column. Every garrison holds: exits sealed, 0211 poisoned, line unbroken.

Last turn. 106 and 113 exit at 0101 for +9 VP - the policy AI only ever exits trains, so the vacated exit cannot be cashed against me in its final move. 0111 keeps its garrison so 0211 stays odds-poisoned through their last combat phase; the ring stands everywhere else. 147 is home after leading a third of their army in circles for half the game.
